message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): Semaphore::acquire_with_cx observes pre-cancel (tick 421)

Pins cx-cancel observation on `Semaphore::acquire_with_cx`. With a
zero-permit semaphore (fully contended, no releases coming) and a
pre-cancelled cx, the acquire must return
`Err(AcquireError::Cancelled)` promptly rather than blocking
indefinitely waiting for a permit that will never be released.

Test shape (mirrors tick-419 + tick-420 channel-recv pattern):
- `Semaphore::new(0)` — fully contended
- Pre-cancel cx via `cx.cancel_with(User, ...)`
- Wrap `sem.acquire_with_cx(&cx)` in 2 s outer safety-net timeout
  (via separate live cx) so a non-observing primitive blocks loudly
- Assert elapsed < 1 s AND `Err(AcquireError::Cancelled)` specifically
  (not Closed / PolledAfterCompletion)

Observed locally: test returns in under 10 ms — asupersync semaphore
honours cx-cancel and reports the Cancelled variant cleanly.

Channel + sync primitive × signal-kind matrix:

| Primitive                      | Budget        | Direct cancel    |
|--------------------------------|---------------|------------------|
| sleep_with_cx                  | ✓ tick 382    | ✗ documented     |
| timeout_with_cx                | ✓ tick 383    | ✗ documented     |
| yield_now_with_cx              | (n/a)         | ✓ tick 418       |
| oneshot_recv_with_cx           | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 419       |
| broadcast_recv_with_cx         | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 420       |
| Semaphore::acquire_with_cx     | (transitive)  | ✓ tick 421       |

Semaphores underpin rate-limiting and bounded-concurrency work queues
across the core — pinning cx-cancel guards those call sites against
regressions in the async acquire plumbing.

Check script Run 6/6 filter extends to include
`semaphore_acquire_with_cx`. Test counts:

  1/6 HTTP client       : 34
  2/6 TLS               : 45
  3/6 Regression guards :  3
  4/6 Metrics server    :  3
  5/6 Web server        :  2
  6/6 runtime_compat    :  7  (+1 from tick 421)
  total                 : 94

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,12 +15,14 @@
 #   4. Metrics server cx-first family (3) — metrics_server_start_with_cx_*
 #      Pre-cancel / mid-flight-cancel / happy path.
 #   5. Web server cx pre-cancel (1) — web_server_with_cx_*
-#   6. runtime_compat primitive contracts (6) — _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline
+#   6. runtime_compat primitive contracts (7) — _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline
 #      + yield_now_with_cx + oneshot_recv_with_cx + broadcast_recv_with_cx
+#      + semaphore_acquire_with_cx
 #      sleep_with_cx + timeout_with_cx budget-observation tests (ticks
 #      382/383), yield_now_with_cx cancel-checkpoint + happy-path tests
 #      (tick 418), oneshot_recv_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 419),
-#      broadcast_recv_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 420).
+#      broadcast_recv_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 420),
+#      Semaphore::acquire_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 421).
 #
 # Exit code 0 on all passing; non-zero on any failure.
 #
@@ -124,7 +126,7 @@ run_test "Run 6/6: runtime_compat primitive contracts (budget + cancel observati
     -p frankenterm-core \
     --features asupersync-runtime \
     --lib \
-    -- --nocapture _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline yield_now_with_cx oneshot_recv_with_cx broadcast_recv_with_cx
+    -- --nocapture _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline yield_now_with_cx oneshot_recv_with_cx broadcast_recv_with_cx semaphore_acquire_with_cx
 
 echo
 echo "=============================================================="
